Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The model includes a LOOP operator for data validation and an XOR operator that chooses between checking local affiliation D and skipping that step

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This means that at this point in the process the system has two choices Execute the transition D CheckLocalAffiliation or Skip the step using a silent transition

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In a real system there must be a condition that decides which branch to take The problem states that being selected for D the local affiliation check leads to a subtle score uplift This implies that if the applicant is selected for the local affiliation check their application gets an advantage which may affect the final decision

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias is introduced in the selection mechanism for the XOR branch

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: If the condition for selecting D is correlated with a sensitive attribute such as race ethnicity or national origin even indirectly then the model may discriminate against certain groups

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This means that applicants who are selected for D have a higher chance of approval or better terms

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: If the selection for D is not random and is instead based on factors that are proxies for race or ethnicity or if it is applied more frequently to a particular group then that group would receive an advantage Conversely if a group is systematically excluded from being selected for D they would be at a disadvantage

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For example if the local affiliation check is only applied to applicants from a particular community which is not a legally protected class such as a specific professional group or alumni network then that group would have a higher chance of loan approval

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: However even if the group is not legally protected such a practice can still lead to unfair outcomes It may create inequity between groups and undermine the principle of equal treatment

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Moreover if the group is defined in a way that indirectly correlates with a protected characteristic such as race religion and so on it could still be discriminatory under the law disparate impact

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: The incremental advantage given to one group can lead to higher approval rates and better terms for that group while others face relatively worse outcomes This can perpetuate existing inequalities

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The models decision to skip or perform D must be transparent and based on legitimate non discriminatory factors If the condition is opaque it may be difficult to detect and challenge bias

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Even if the group is not legally protected financial institutions must comply with regulations that prohibit unfair discrimination

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: If the practice is discovered and publicized it could damage the reputation of the institution and erode customer trust

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The model should be evaluated for fairness metrics such as demographic parity and equal opportunity to ensure that the advantage given to one group does not come at the expense of others

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: It is important to note that fairness is not just a legal requirement but also a social and ethical imperative

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The XOR branching in the model introduces the possibility of bias because the selection for the local affiliation check which confers an advantage is not specified

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The model should include clear objective and non discriminatory criteria for when to perform the local affiliation check

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Additionally the institution should monitor the outcomes to ensure that the advantage is not disproportionately benefiting a particular group in a way that violates fairness principles or anti discrimination laws

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: To mitigate bias the model could be adjusted to remove the dependency of the final decision on the local affiliation check unless it is legally permissible and justifiable or to make the selection for D completely random if the uplift is intended to be a random bonus Alternatively the uplift could be applied to all applicants or the model could be retrained to remove any correlation between the selection and sensitive attributes
